cards.boundary_note: Educational only. Banking policy and consumer-finance actions can depend on jurisdiction, account terms, and current rules, so use primary regulators or qualified professionals for decisions.
core_idea: Money is useful because people expect others to accept it later, while credit extends that trust across time.
Banks sit between present cash and future repayment, so the banking system is partly plumbing and partly a confidence network.
cards.example: A paycheck deposit, a card payment, and a mortgage all rely on promises being recorded and settled.
cards.key_takeaway: Banking starts with trusted records of who can pay whom.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Banking starts with trusted records of who can pay whom.
review_prompts.prompt: In your own words, explain "Money, credit, and trust" and name one way someone could misunderstand it.

review_prompts.answer_hint: A paycheck deposit, a card payment, and a mortgage all rely on promises being recorded and settled.

cards.title: Money, credit, and trust

core_idea: A commercial bank is a private institution that takes deposits, makes loans, provides payment access, and manages risk.
It earns by transforming funding into assets while meeting rules, withdrawals, and payment obligations.
cards.example: A neighborhood bank account is both a service to the customer and a funding source for the bank.
cards.key_takeaway: A bank is not a vault; it is a regulated balance-sheet business.

review_prompts.answer_hint: A bank is not a vault; it is a regulated balance-sheet business.

cards.boundary_note: Educational only. Banking policy and consumer-finance actions can depend on jurisdiction, account terms, and current rules, so use primary regulators or qualified professionals for decisions.
core_idea: A deposit feels like your asset, but on the bank balance sheet it is money the bank owes you.
That liability must be honored through withdrawals, transfers, or payments even though the bank has invested or lent much of the funding.
cards.example: When you see 500 dollars in checking, the bank records a matching obligation to let you spend it.
cards.key_takeaway: Deposits are promises by the bank, not labeled bundles of cash in a drawer.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Deposits are promises by the bank, not labeled bundles of cash in a drawer.

core_idea: A payment system is the rulebook and infrastructure that moves claims between people, banks, merchants, and central-bank accounts.
The visible tap or transfer hides authorization, clearing, settlement, fraud controls, and final record updates.
cards.example: Buying groceries with a debit card can involve a merchant bank, card network, your bank, and settlement records.
cards.key_takeaway: Payments are coordinated record changes, not just money flying through wires.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Payments are coordinated record changes, not just money flying through wires.

core_idea: A bank balance sheet lists assets such as loans and securities against liabilities such as deposits and wholesale funding.
Capital is the loss-absorbing cushion between asset values and promises owed to others.
cards.example: If loans lose value, capital takes the first hit before depositors or other creditors are threatened.
cards.key_takeaway: Reading bank strength begins with assets, liabilities, and capital.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Reading bank strength begins with assets, liabilities, and capital.

core_idea: Fractional-reserve banking means banks keep only part of deposit funding as immediately available reserves or cash.
The system works when withdrawals are ordinary and confidence holds, but it becomes fragile when many depositors demand liquidity at once.
cards.example: A bank can safely serve normal ATM withdrawals while still funding long-term loans.
cards.key_takeaway: Banks need enough liquidity for expected demands, not one-for-one cash for every deposit.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Banks need enough liquidity for expected demands, not one-for-one cash for every deposit.

Prepares for Misconception: banks only lend pre-existing deposits.

core_idea: When a bank makes a loan, it usually credits the borrower's deposit account, creating a new bank deposit alongside a loan asset.
The loan still requires capital, funding, risk controls, and settlement capacity, so creation is constrained rather than magical.
cards.example: A business loan may appear as new spendable balance before anyone else has reduced their checking account.
cards.key_takeaway: Bank lending can expand deposits, but it is limited by risk, regulation, capital, and demand.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Bank lending can expand deposits, but it is limited by risk, regulation, capital, and demand.

review_prompts.answer_hint: A business loan may appear as new spendable balance before anyone else has reduced their checking account.

core_idea: Credit risk is the chance that borrowers do not repay as agreed, reducing the value of the bank's loan assets.
Banks price, diversify, collateralize, and provision for this risk because bad loans can consume capital.
cards.example: A mortgage and an unsecured business loan expose the bank to very different repayment uncertainties.
cards.key_takeaway: Every loan is also a prediction about future repayment.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Every loan is also a prediction about future repayment.

core_idea: An interest rate compensates lenders for waiting, expected inflation, uncertainty, and borrower risk.
Different rates coexist because loans differ in maturity, collateral, liquidity, tax treatment, and default probability.
cards.example: A short Treasury bill and a risky credit-card balance do not carry the same rate because they are not the same promise.
cards.key_takeaway: Interest rates are prices attached to time, risk, and money conditions.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Interest rates are prices attached to time, risk, and money conditions.

core_idea: Net interest margin is the spread between what a bank earns on assets and what it pays for funding.
It connects customer rates, central-bank policy, deposit competition, loan quality, and profitability.
cards.example: If deposit rates rise faster than loan yields, a bank's margin can shrink even while rates are high.
cards.key_takeaway: A bank's profit depends on the spread, not just whether rates are high.

review_prompts.answer_hint: A bank's profit depends on the spread, not just whether rates are high.

core_idea: Reserves are balances banks hold at the central bank and use to settle payments with one another.
They are not the same as capital or customer deposits; they are a special settlement asset inside the banking system.
cards.example: When your bank sends money to another bank, reserve balances may move behind the scenes.
cards.key_takeaway: Reserves are the banking system's settlement money.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Reserves are the banking system's settlement money.

core_idea: Interbank settlement is the final adjustment of obligations between banks after customers make payments.
Settlement reduces a web of promises to authoritative records, often using central-bank money for finality.
cards.example: Thousands of customer transfers can net to a smaller movement between two banks at the end of a cycle.
cards.key_takeaway: Payments become durable when banks settle with each other.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Payments become durable when banks settle with each other.

core_idea: Clearing houses help participants calculate obligations, manage risk, and prepare transactions for settlement.
Finality matters because businesses need to know when a payment cannot be unwound except through a new transaction.
cards.example: A merchant wants confidence that a paid invoice will not vanish after goods are released.
cards.key_takeaway: Clearing organizes obligations; finality makes payment records dependable.

core_idea: Liquidity is the ability to meet near-term cash demands, while solvency means assets are worth more than liabilities.
A solvent institution can fail if it cannot get cash in time, and a liquid institution can still be insolvent if losses are hidden.
cards.example: A homeowner with valuable property but no cash for today's bill has a liquidity problem, not necessarily a net-worth problem.
cards.key_takeaway: Do not confuse having cash today with being economically sound.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Do not confuse having cash today with being economically sound.

core_idea: Maturity transformation means funding long-term assets such as loans with shorter-term liabilities such as deposits.
It supports useful lending but creates rollover and withdrawal risk because promised payment times do not perfectly match.
cards.example: A bank may fund a 30-year mortgage partly with deposits customers can move tomorrow.
cards.key_takeaway: Banking creates value by transforming maturities, and fragility by mismatching them.

core_idea: A bank run happens when many funders try to withdraw because each fears being late to the exit.
Even rumors can become self-reinforcing when depositors believe others will withdraw first.
cards.example: A solvent bank can be forced to sell assets quickly at losses if everyone demands cash at once.
cards.key_takeaway: Runs are coordination failures around confidence and liquidity.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Runs are coordination failures around confidence and liquidity.

core_idea: Deposit insurance protects eligible deposits up to defined limits and reduces the incentive for small depositors to run.
It supports confidence but also requires supervision and limits because protection can weaken market discipline.
cards.example: An insured household checking account is treated differently from an uninsured corporate balance above the limit.
cards.key_takeaway: Deposit insurance calms runs by making many depositors less exposed.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Deposit insurance calms runs by making many depositors less exposed.

core_idea: A central-bank mandate states the public goals it is expected to pursue, such as price stability, employment, financial stability, or payments safety.
Mandates differ by country and create tradeoffs when goals point in different directions.

cards.key_takeaway: Central banks act through legal mandates, not personal preference alone.

core_idea: Monetary policy transmission is the chain from central-bank tools to financial conditions, spending, hiring, and inflation.
The chain is indirect because households, banks, firms, markets, and expectations all react with delays.
cards.example: A policy-rate change may affect mortgage rates, which affects home buying, construction, and eventually prices.
cards.key_takeaway: Central banks steer through channels, not direct remote control.

core_idea: A policy rate is a short-term anchor, while a yield curve shows interest rates across maturities.
Longer rates include expectations about future short rates, inflation, risk, and term premiums.
cards.example: Mortgage rates may move even before a meeting if markets expect future policy changes.
cards.key_takeaway: The central bank strongly influences short rates, while markets help price the rest of the curve.

core_idea: Open market operations are central-bank purchases or sales of securities used to manage reserves and short-term rates.
They work by changing the supply of settlement balances and the prices of safe assets in financial markets.
cards.example: Buying Treasury securities can add reserves to banks and help keep the overnight rate near target.
cards.key_takeaway: Open market operations are practical plumbing for policy implementation.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Open market operations are practical plumbing for policy implementation.

core_idea: Reserve requirements set a minimum share of certain liabilities banks must hold in reserve-eligible form.
They are one possible constraint on bank balance sheets, but modern systems often rely more on capital, liquidity rules, and interest on reserves.
cards.example: A country may change reserve requirements without that being the main driver of everyday loan supply.
cards.key_takeaway: Reserve requirements matter, but they are not the whole story of credit creation.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Reserve requirements matter, but they are not the whole story of credit creation.

core_idea: Standing facilities let banks borrow from or deposit with the central bank under defined terms.
They form guardrails around short-term interest rates and provide backup liquidity when markets are strained.
cards.example: A discount window loan can help a bank meet temporary payment needs without dumping assets.
cards.key_takeaway: Standing facilities are safety valves for short-term money markets.

core_idea: A lender of last resort provides emergency liquidity to solvent institutions facing panic or market breakdown.
The idea is to stop contagious runs while avoiding bailouts of institutions that are fundamentally insolvent.
cards.example: A central bank may lend against good collateral during a panic so payments keep functioning.
cards.key_takeaway: Last-resort lending is about containing panic without erasing discipline.

core_idea: Inflation is a sustained rise in the general price level, and money demand is the public's desire to hold liquid balances.
The relationship between money and prices depends on output, velocity, expectations, supply shocks, and policy credibility.
cards.example: Prices can rise after supply disruptions even if households are not suddenly richer.
cards.key_takeaway: Money matters for inflation, but the channel is broader than simple printing.

core_idea: Deflation can raise the real burden of fixed debts because borrowers repay in money that buys more than expected.
That pressure can reduce spending, increase defaults, and make downturns harder to escape.
cards.example: A business with fixed loan payments may struggle if its selling prices fall across the economy.
cards.key_takeaway: Falling prices can be dangerous when debts are fixed in nominal terms.

core_idea: Quantitative easing is large-scale central-bank asset buying used when ordinary short-term-rate tools are constrained.
It aims to affect longer-term yields, market functioning, portfolios, and expectations, but its effects are debated and context-dependent.
cards.example: Buying long-term government bonds can push investors toward other assets and lower borrowing costs.
cards.key_takeaway: QE is an unconventional policy tool, not a simple cash drop to households.

review_prompts.answer_hint: QE is an unconventional policy tool, not a simple cash drop to households.

core_idea: Forward guidance is communication about likely future policy used to shape expectations today.
Because financial prices reflect expected future conditions, credible guidance can affect rates before any tool is changed.
cards.example: A central bank statement that rates may stay high longer can move bond yields immediately.
cards.key_takeaway: Expectations are a policy channel when people believe the message.

connections.label: Prepares for Reading bank news with a systems map.

core_idea: Bank capital is the owner's loss-absorbing claim that stands between asset losses and many creditors.
More capital makes a bank more resilient, but it can also affect returns, lending incentives, and funding costs.
cards.example: If a loan portfolio loses value, equity absorbs losses before insured depositors are affected.
cards.key_takeaway: Capital is the banking system's shock absorber.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Capital is the banking system's shock absorber.
review_prompts.prompt: In your own words, explain "Bank capital" and name one way someone could misunderstand it.

review_prompts.answer_hint: If a loan portfolio loses value, equity absorbs losses before insured depositors are affected.

cards.title: Bank capital

cards.boundary_note: Educational only. Banking policy and consumer-finance actions can depend on jurisdiction, account terms, and current rules, so use primary regulators or qualified professionals for decisions.
core_idea: Leverage means using borrowed or promised funds to hold assets larger than the owner's capital.
It magnifies gains when assets perform and magnifies losses when they do not, so a small drop in asset value can wipe out a leveraged owner's thin capital cushion.
cards.example: A bank with 5 dollars of capital supporting 100 dollars of assets has little room for asset losses.
cards.key_takeaway: Leverage makes banking powerful and fragile at the same time.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Leverage makes banking powerful and fragile at the same time.
review_prompts.prompt: In your own words, explain "Leverage" and name one way someone could misunderstand it.

review_prompts.answer_hint: A bank with 5 dollars of capital supporting 100 dollars of assets has little room for asset losses.
review_prompts.prompt: Look at the example for "Leverage". What detail does it show that the definition alone would miss?

cards.title: Leverage

cards.boundary_note: Educational only. Banking policy and consumer-finance actions can depend on jurisdiction, account terms, and current rules, so use primary regulators or qualified professionals for decisions.
core_idea: Capital requirements set minimum loss-absorbing resources banks must hold relative to assets or risk-weighted exposures.
They force resilience into private balance sheets before stress arrives, though measurement choices matter.
cards.example: A riskier loan book generally requires more capital than a portfolio of safer government securities.
cards.key_takeaway: Capital rules turn the idea of resilience into a measurable constraint.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Capital rules turn the idea of resilience into a measurable constraint.
review_prompts.prompt: In your own words, explain "Capital requirements" and name one way someone could misunderstand it.

review_prompts.answer_hint: A riskier loan book generally requires more capital than a portfolio of safer government securities.

cards.title: Capital requirements

cards.boundary_note: Educational only. Banking policy and consumer-finance actions can depend on jurisdiction, account terms, and current rules, so use primary regulators or qualified professionals for decisions.
core_idea: Stress tests ask whether a bank could survive severe but plausible economic scenarios.
They reveal vulnerabilities that ordinary good times may hide and can require banks to adjust capital plans.
cards.example: A regulator might test what happens if unemployment jumps and housing prices fall together.
cards.key_takeaway: Stress tests are rehearsals for financial weather that has not arrived yet.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Stress tests are rehearsals for financial weather that has not arrived yet.

review_prompts.answer_hint: A regulator might test what happens if unemployment jumps and housing prices fall together.

cards.boundary_note: Educational only. Banking policy and consumer-finance actions can depend on jurisdiction, account terms, and current rules, so use primary regulators or qualified professionals for decisions.
core_idea: Macroprudential regulation focuses on risks to the financial system as a whole rather than one institution at a time.
It watches shared exposures, feedback loops, procyclical lending, and contagion channels.
cards.example: If many lenders loosen mortgage standards together, each may look fine alone while the system becomes fragile.
cards.key_takeaway: System safety requires seeing connections, not just individual bank health.

review_prompts.answer_hint: System safety requires seeing connections, not just individual bank health.

cards.boundary_note: Educational only. Banking policy and consumer-finance actions can depend on jurisdiction, account terms, and current rules, so use primary regulators or qualified professionals for decisions.
core_idea: Shadow banking describes credit intermediation outside traditional deposit-taking banks.
It can provide useful funding but may recreate bank-like run risks without the same protections or visibility.
cards.example: Money market funds and securitization vehicles can fund credit while sitting outside ordinary bank deposits.
cards.key_takeaway: Bank-like fragility can appear outside institutions called banks.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Bank-like fragility can appear outside institutions called banks.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Money market funds and securitization vehicles can fund credit while sitting outside ordinary bank deposits.

connections.label: Builds on How lending creates deposits.

core_idea: Securitization pools financial assets such as loans and turns their cash flows into tradable securities.
It can spread risk and funding sources, but complexity can hide who ultimately bears losses.
cards.example: A bundle of mortgages can be converted into bonds bought by investors around the world.
cards.key_takeaway: Securitization changes loans from local relationships into market instruments.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Securitization changes loans from local relationships into market instruments.

cards.boundary_note: Educational only. Banking policy and consumer-finance actions can depend on jurisdiction, account terms, and current rules, so use primary regulators or qualified professionals for decisions.
core_idea: Central bank independence means policy decisions are insulated from short-term political pressure within a legal mandate.
Independence can support credibility, but it must be balanced by accountability, transparency, and democratic legitimacy.
cards.example: A central bank may resist pressure to keep rates low before an election if inflation risks are high.
cards.key_takeaway: Independence is a governance design for credibility, not freedom from accountability.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Independence is a governance design for credibility, not freedom from accountability.
review_prompts.prompt: In your own words, explain "Central bank independence" and name one way someone could misunderstand it.

review_prompts.answer_hint: A central bank may resist pressure to keep rates low before an election if inflation risks are high.

cards.boundary_note: Educational only. Banking policy and consumer-finance actions can depend on jurisdiction, account terms, and current rules, so use primary regulators or qualified professionals for decisions.
core_idea: Exchange rates connect domestic policy to global capital flows, trade prices, and imported inflation.
Central banks may influence currencies through rates, interventions, or communication, but market forces and foreign policy also matter.
cards.example: Higher domestic rates can attract capital and strengthen a currency, all else equal.
cards.key_takeaway: A central bank operates in a world of cross-border money.

review_prompts.answer_hint: A central bank operates in a world of cross-border money.

core_idea: Correspondent banking lets one bank provide services for another, often across borders or currencies.
It expands payment reach but introduces compliance, settlement, and concentration risks.
cards.example: A small bank may use a larger international bank to process foreign-currency payments.
cards.key_takeaway: Global payments often rely on bank relationships behind the customer interface.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Global payments often rely on bank relationships behind the customer interface.

core_idea: Money laundering controls aim to prevent financial services from hiding criminal proceeds or sanctioned activity.
Banks must know customers, monitor suspicious patterns, and report certain activity while avoiding unfair exclusion.
cards.example: A bank may ask for business ownership documents before opening an account.

core_idea: Consumer protection rules address disclosure, fairness, error resolution, and abusive practices in financial services.
They matter because banking products are complex and mistakes can harm household stability.
cards.example: A clear fee disclosure helps a customer compare accounts before overdrafts become costly.
cards.key_takeaway: Trust in banking depends on fair treatment as well as financial soundness.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Trust in banking depends on fair treatment as well as financial soundness.

connections.label: Builds on How lending creates deposits.

core_idea: The simple story that banks lend only money someone already deposited misses how bank lending can create deposits.
The better map still includes constraints: capital, risk, demand, regulation, funding, and settlement all limit lending.
cards.example: A new business loan can create a deposit balance, but the bank must still manage the resulting risk and payments.
cards.key_takeaway: Credit creation is constrained balance-sheet expansion, not a pile of old cash being passed along.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Credit creation is constrained balance-sheet expansion, not a pile of old cash being passed along.
review_prompts.prompt: In your own words, explain "Misconception: banks only lend pre-existing deposits" and name one way someone could misunderstand it.

review_prompts.answer_hint: A new business loan can create a deposit balance, but the bank must still manage the resulting risk and payments.

connections.label: Builds on Policy rates and yield curves.

core_idea: Central banks directly target or administer some short-term rates, but many market rates are priced by expectations and risk.
Policy influence travels through curves, spreads, banks, and markets rather than commanding every loan price.
cards.example: A credit-card rate can stay high even after a policy cut because default risk and pricing practices differ.
cards.key_takeaway: Central-bank influence is strong but not mechanical control of every rate.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Central-bank influence is strong but not mechanical control of every rate.
review_prompts.prompt: In your own words, explain "Misconception: central banks directly set every interest rate" and name one way someone could misunderstand it.

review_prompts.answer_hint: A credit-card rate can stay high even after a policy cut because default risk and pricing practices differ.

cards.title: Misconception: central banks directly set every interest rate

cards.boundary_note: Educational only. Banking policy and consumer-finance actions can depend on jurisdiction, account terms, and current rules, so use primary regulators or qualified professionals for decisions.

connections.label: Builds on Inflation and money demand.

core_idea: More money can contribute to inflation, but the result depends on output, demand, velocity, expectations, and supply conditions.
A richer model asks whether new money changes spending pressure relative to the economy's ability to produce.
cards.example: Emergency liquidity in a frozen market may not affect prices the same way as sustained deficit monetization during overheating.
cards.key_takeaway: Money growth matters through context, not through an automatic one-step rule.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Money growth matters through context, not through an automatic one-step rule.
review_prompts.prompt: In your own words, explain "Misconception: printing money automatically causes inflation" and name one way someone could misunderstand it.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Emergency liquidity in a frozen market may not affect prices the same way as sustained deficit monetization during overheating.

cards.boundary_note: Educational only. Banking policy and consumer-finance actions can depend on jurisdiction, account terms, and current rules, so use primary regulators or qualified professionals for decisions.
core_idea: Price stability focuses on inflation, while financial stability focuses on resilient institutions and markets.
The goals often support each other but can conflict when inflation is high and markets are fragile.
cards.example: Raising rates may cool prices while also exposing leveraged borrowers or banks with interest-rate losses.
cards.key_takeaway: Central-bank tradeoffs are real when different forms of stability pull apart.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Central-bank tradeoffs are real when different forms of stability pull apart.
review_prompts.prompt: In your own words, explain "Financial stability versus price stability" and name one way someone could misunderstand it.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Raising rates may cool prices while also exposing leveraged borrowers or banks with interest-rate losses.

core_idea: A central bank digital currency would be a public digital money design, not simply a private payment app.
Design choices about privacy, bank funding, offline use, and financial inclusion determine its effects.
cards.example: A retail CBDC could let households hold a digital claim on the central bank, depending on national design.
cards.key_takeaway: CBDCs are policy designs with tradeoffs, not just new payment technology.

review_prompts.answer_hint: CBDCs are policy designs with tradeoffs, not just new payment technology.

core_idea: Real-time payments move funds and confirmations near instantly, changing expectations for speed and availability.
They require fraud controls, liquidity management, and clear finality rules because mistakes can also move faster.
cards.example: An instant bill payment helps a household avoid a late fee, but a scam transfer may be harder to reverse.
cards.key_takeaway: Faster payments increase convenience and the need for stronger safeguards.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Faster payments increase convenience and the need for stronger safeguards.

core_idea: Bank profitability changes with interest-rate cycles, credit losses, competition, and regulation.
A good lending year can hide future losses because defaults often arrive after economic conditions turn.
cards.example: Profits may look strong when loan growth is rapid, then weaken when borrowers struggle.
cards.key_takeaway: Bank earnings are cyclical because credit quality and funding costs move with the economy.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Bank earnings are cyclical because credit quality and funding costs move with the economy.

core_idea: Crisis resolution is the process for handling a failing financial institution while limiting wider damage.
Tools can include sale, bridge bank, bail-in, liquidation, guarantees, or emergency liquidity depending on law and facts.
cards.example: A regulator may transfer insured deposits to another bank over a weekend to preserve access.
cards.key_takeaway: Resolution tries to protect critical functions without pretending losses disappeared.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Resolution tries to protect critical functions without pretending losses disappeared.

core_idea: Moral hazard appears when protection changes behavior by reducing the cost of taking risk.
Deposit insurance and emergency lending can stabilize the system, but safeguards are needed so risk-takers do not expect rescue.
cards.example: If managers believe creditors will always be protected, they may accept more leverage than society wants.
cards.key_takeaway: Safety nets need rules because protection can change incentives.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Safety nets need rules because protection can change incentives.

review_prompts.answer_hint: If managers believe creditors will always be protected, they may accept more leverage than society wants.

cards.boundary_note: Educational only. Banking policy and consumer-finance actions can depend on jurisdiction, account terms, and current rules, so use primary regulators or qualified professionals for decisions.
core_idea: Banking news is easier to read when you separate liquidity, solvency, capital, funding, payments, policy, and confidence.
The same headline may involve several layers, so a systems map prevents one dramatic detail from becoming the whole explanation.
cards.example: A story about emergency lending may be about liquidity support, not necessarily a declaration that every borrower is insolvent.
cards.key_takeaway: Good banking analysis asks which layer of the system is under stress.

review_prompts.answer_hint: Good banking analysis asks which layer of the system is under stress.
